text.title: mTLS Service-to-Service Deployment Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in production. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: 20 services share `client.crt` mounted from a single K8s Secret; one compromised pod = revocation of all services.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated mTLS as a simple shared-secret model.
antipattern.fix: Issue per-service identity via SPIFFE / cert-manager + CSI driver; each workload gets its own short-lived cert; remove the shared Secret.

antipattern.symptom: Service client cert valid for 5 years; rotation never happens; key compromise unrecoverable within window.
antipattern.fix: Reduce lifetime to ≤24h; automate renewal via SPIRE workload API or cert-manager; verify rotation works under load.

antipattern.symptom: nginx config sets `ssl_verify_client optional` and downstream code never checks `$ssl_client_s_dn`; mTLS is fiction.
antipattern.root-cause: Author left a debug `optional` setting and forgot to enforce.
antipattern.fix: Set `ssl_verify_client on; ssl_verify_depth 2;` and gate request forwarding on a non-empty client DN.
